agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Parcel delivery logistics platform managing shipment lifecycle from creation through delivery, with weight-based pricing tiers. Core workflow: create shipment → transit → delivered, with strict state machine constraints and weight validation.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A domain entity representing a parcel moving from a pickup address to a delivery address, progressing through defined lifecycle states."},{"term":"Pickup Address","definition":"The origin location where a parcel is collected. Required at shipment creation."},{"term":"Delivery Address","definition":"The destination location where a parcel must be delivered. Required at shipment creation."},{"term":"Shipment State","definition":"The lifecycle phase of a shipment. Valid states: Pending, In Transit, Delivered. Transitions are unidirectional."},{"term":"Pending","definition":"Initial state of a newly created shipment, before it begins moving."},{"term":"In Transit","definition":"State indicating the shipment is actively being transported. Irreversible — cannot return to Pending."},{"term":"Delivered","definition":"Terminal state indicating the shipment has reached its delivery address. No further state transitions allowed."},{"term":"Weight","definition":"The physical mass of the parcel in kilograms. Must be positive and at most 1000 kg."},{"term":"Weight Bracket","definition":"A pricing classification derived from shipment weight: Standard (0–10 kg], Heavy (10–50 kg], Freight (>50 kg)."},{"term":"Standard","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments weighing more than 0 kg up to and including 10 kg."},{"term":"Heavy","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments weighing more than 10 kg up to and including 50 kg."},{"term":"Freight","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments weighing more than 50 kg up to and including 1000 kg."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A shipment requires both a pickup address and a delivery address at creation time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A shipment without both endpoints is physically meaningless — there is no parcel movement without an origin and destination. This is a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to create a shipment missing a pickup address or delivery address must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Shipment state transitions are unidirectional: Pending → In Transit → Delivered. No backward transitions are permitted.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Reversing state would corrupt the shipment audit trail and violate the physical reality of logistics progression. A parcel that has left the warehouse cannot be un-dispatched.","testable_assertion":"A shipment in state In Transit cannot transition to Pending. A shipment in state Delivered cannot transition to any other state."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Delivered is a terminal state — no further state changes are allowed on a delivered shipment.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Once a parcel is delivered, the business obligation is fulfilled. Allowing state changes would corrupt billing records, tracking history, and proof-of-delivery integrity.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to change the state of a Delivered shipment must be rejected regardless of the target state."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Shipment weight must be strictly positive (greater than zero).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A shipment with zero or negative weight is physically impossible and would break weight-based pricing calculations.","testable_assertion":"Any shipment with weight <= 0 must be rejected at creation and at any update."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Shipment weight must not exceed 1000 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 1000 kg cap is a business policy constraint, likely driven by vehicle capacity, insurance limits, or operational capability. A VP could raise this to 1500 kg if the fleet changes.","testable_assertion":"Any shipment with weight > 1000 kg must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Weight bracket classification: Standard = (0, 10] kg, Heavy = (10, 50] kg, Freight = (50, 1000] kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Bracket thresholds are pricing policy. Marketing or finance could adjust these thresholds (e.g., Standard up to 15 kg) without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"A 10 kg shipment is classified as Standard. A 10.01 kg shipment is classified as Heavy. A 50 kg shipment is classified as Heavy. A 50.01 kg shipment is classified as Freight."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"A newly created shipment begins in the Pending state.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Skipping Pending would mean a shipment is created already in transit or delivered, violating the sequential physical reality of pickup → transport → delivery.","testable_assertion":"After successful creation, a shipment's state must be Pending."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A request to create a shipment with a valid pickup address, delivery address, and weight of 5 kg","when":"The shipment is created","then":"The shipment is persisted in Pending state with weight bracket Standard"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A request to create a shipment with a missing pickup address","when":"The creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating pickup address is required"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A request to create a shipment with a missing delivery address","when":"The creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating delivery address is required"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A shipment in Pending state","when":"It is transitioned to In Transit","then":"The state changes to In Transit successfully"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A shipment in In Transit state","when":"An attempt is made to transition it back to Pending","then":"The system rejects the transition and the shipment remains In Transit"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A shipment in Delivered state","when":"An attempt is made to change its state to any other state","then":"The system rejects the transition and the shipment remains Delivered"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A request to create a shipment with weight 0 kg","when":"The creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating weight must be positive"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A request to create a shipment with weight -5 kg","when":"The creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating weight must be positive"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A request to create a shipment with weight 1001 kg","when":"The creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request indicating weight exceeds the 1000 kg maximum"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A shipment with weight exactly 10 kg","when":"The weight bracket is determined","then":"The bracket is Standard"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A shipment with weight 10.01 kg","when":"The weight bracket is determined","then":"The bracket is Heavy"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A shipment with weight exactly 50 kg","when":"The weight bracket is determined","then":"The bracket is Heavy"},{"id":"AC-13","given":"A shipment with weight 50.01 kg","when":"The weight bracket is determined","then":"The bracket is Freight"},{"id":"AC-14","given":"A shipment in Pending state","when":"An attempt is made to transition directly to Delivered","then":"The system rejects the transition — shipments must pass through In Transit first"},{"id":"AC-15","given":"A shipment with weight exactly 1000 kg","when":"The shipment is created","then":"The shipment is accepted and classified as Freight"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Weight at exact bracket boundaries (10 kg, 50 kg)","expected_behavior":"10 kg → Standard, 50 kg → Heavy. Boundary values belong to the lower bracket (inclusive upper bound)."},{"scenario":"Pending → Delivered direct transition (skipping In Transit)","expected_behavior":"Must be rejected. The spec implies a sequential state machine: Pending → In Transit → Delivered. Skipping states violates the physical delivery process."},{"scenario":"Weight of 0.001 kg (extremely small but positive)","expected_behavior":"Accepted. Weight is positive and within range. Classified as Standard."},{"scenario":"Weight of exactly 1000 kg","expected_behavior":"Accepted as Freight. This is the upper boundary of the allowed range."},{"scenario":"Shipment created with valid addresses but missing weight","expected_behavior":"Should be rejected — weight is required for bracket classification and billing. Spec implies weight is mandatory."},{"scenario":"Attempt to modify pickup or delivery address after shipment is Delivered","expected_behavior":"Should be rejected — Delivered is a terminal state and the spec says state cannot change. Whether address immutability extends beyond state is ambiguous (see escalations)."},{"scenario":"Concurrent state transitions on the same shipment","expected_behavior":"Only one transition should succeed; the system must enforce state consistency to prevent double-delivery or conflicting states."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Weight bracket classification directly determines pricing. Incorrect bracket assignment causes revenue leakage (undercharging) or customer disputes (overcharging). Terminal state enforcement protects billing finality — reopening delivered shipments could trigger refund fraud.","operational":"Strict state machine prevents operational chaos: dispatchers cannot accidentally revert in-transit parcels to pending, and delivered parcels cannot re-enter the pipeline. Weight cap of 1000 kg likely aligns with fleet and handling capacity.","user":"Customers rely on shipment state for tracking visibility. State integrity ensures tracking accuracy. Clear rejection messages on invalid weight or missing addresses prevent frustration during shipment creation.","risk":"Primary risks: (1) bracket boundary bugs causing systematic mis-pricing, (2) state machine bypass allowing delivered shipments to be modified (audit/compliance risk), (3) weight validation gaps allowing impossible shipments into the system.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Shippers (customers creating shipments)","impact":"Depend on clear validation feedback at creation and accurate weight-based pricing."},{"group":"Recipients","impact":"Depend on accurate tracking state to know when to expect delivery."},{"group":"Dispatchers / Operations","impact":"Depend on state machine integrity to manage fleet and route planning without corrupted shipment data."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Depend on correct weight bracket assignment for invoicing and on terminal Delivered state for revenue recognition."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Handle disputes arising from incorrect pricing or unexpected state changes."}]},"confidence":{"level":78,"reasoning":"The spec is concise and internally consistent with no contradictions. Core state machine and weight rules are clear. Confidence is not higher because: (1) bracket boundary inclusivity is not explicitly stated (is 10 kg Standard or Heavy?), (2) whether Pending → Delivered skip is allowed is implied but not stated, (3) whether weight can be modified after creation is unspecified, (4) no mention of cancellation as a valid state.","high_confidence_areas":["State machine directionality (no backward transitions)","Delivered is terminal","Weight must be positive and <= 1000 kg","Both addresses required at creation","Three weight brackets exist"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact bracket boundary inclusivity (spec says '0-10', '10-50', 'over 50' — the 10 and 50 boundaries are ambiguous)","Whether Pending can skip directly to Delivered","Whether weight or addresses can be modified after creation","Absence of a Cancelled state — real logistics systems almost always have one"],"assumptions":["Bracket boundaries are inclusive on the upper end for the lower bracket (10 kg = Standard, 50 kg = Heavy) based on conventional bracket notation","Pending → Delivered direct transition is not allowed, as the spec describes a sequential flow","Weight is required at shipment creation","A shipment has exactly one weight value (not multi-parcel)"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Bracket boundary ownership is ambiguous. Spec says '0-10 kg is standard, 10-50 kg is heavy, over 50 kg is freight.' The value 10 kg and 50 kg each appear in two brackets. Which bracket owns the boundary value?","affected_stakeholders":["Finance / Billing","Shippers","Customer Support"],"options":["A: 10 kg = Standard, 50 kg = Heavy (lower bracket owns boundary)","B: 10 kg = Heavy, 50 kg = Freight (upper bracket owns boundary)"],"recommendation":"Option A — conventional pricing favors the customer at boundaries and '0-10 kg is standard' reads as inclusive of 10."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"No Cancelled state is mentioned. Real logistics platforms almost universally support cancellation of shipments before delivery. Is cancellation intentionally excluded or simply omitted?","affected_stakeholders":["Shippers","Operations","Finance / Billing"],"options":["A: No cancellation exists — once created, a shipment must eventually be delivered","B: Cancellation is an allowed transition from Pending and/or In Transit"],"recommendation":"Clarify with product manager. Option B is standard in the industry, and omitting it may cause operational problems."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Can a shipment transition directly from Pending to Delivered, skipping In Transit? The spec says 'once in transit, it cannot be sent back to pending' but does not explicitly define all valid transitions.","affected_stakeholders":["Operations","Recipients"],"options":["A: Only sequential transitions allowed (Pending → In Transit → Delivered)","B: Pending → Delivered is also valid (e.g., same-location pickup/delivery)"],"recommendation":"Option A — the spec implies a sequential lifecycle and skipping In Transit would undermine tracking and operational workflows."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
